utilityName: San Antonio Water System Backflow Prevention
searchAliases: san antonio backflow prevention annual testing
dueBasis: Annual testing is required by City of San Antonio ordinance and state regulations. SAWS uses BSI to manage notices, customer service inspections, and customer tracking.
coveredPropertyTypes: Connections that may present a potential source of contamination to the public water system
Sites requiring internal assemblies to protect customers from hazardous cross-connections
officialListLabel: Find a registered testing company in the SAWS BSI portal
penalties: SAWS monitors and enforces annual testing requirements for external assemblies, and BSI now handles due and past-due notice operations on behalf of SAWS.
sourceExcerpt: SAWS requires annual testing under city ordinance and state regulations and directs customers to BSI to find registered testing companies and check compliance.
verdictSummary: San Antonio requires annual backflow testing and routes both customer compliance checks and registered testing company discovery through BSI under the SAWS program.
whoIsAffected: Properties with connections that can contaminate the public water system and customers requiring external or internal backflow assemblies.
residentialNotes: Residential users most often see SAWS through annual notices for irrigation, fire sprinkler, septic, well, or alternative-water conditions.
SAWS is not a one-size-fits-all page because internal and external assemblies can sit on different compliance tracks.
commercialNotes: Commercial owners should expect SAWS customer service inspection and development workflows to overlap with annual testing when hazard conditions change.
The public materials are clearer on BSI workflow and notice handling than on retail tester pricing.

annualTesting.summary: SAWS requires annual testing under City ordinance and state regulations and now uses BSI for due notices, customer tracking, and tester lookup.
highlights: Annual testing is the default rule for SAWS backflow assemblies.
BSI handles due and past-due notices for SAWS.
A failed annual test form counts as delinquent until the assembly passes.
workflowSteps: Use the BSI customer portal to confirm due status and tester options.
Book the test with a registered SAWS testing company.
Repair and retest promptly if the annual form shows a failure because SAWS treats failed annual tests as delinquent.

irrigation.summary: SAWS irrigation compliance is broader than a single annual test reminder. Irrigation systems need the right assembly, and large landscapes can also fall into the separate annual irrigation checkup program.
highlights: SAWS requires backflow protection on irrigation systems based on the hazard presented.
Single-family irrigation tied to septic systems, private wells, or other alternate sources often sits in the annual notice stream.
Large landscapes over five acres or very high annual water use may also need an annual irrigation checkup by May 1.
workflowSteps: Confirm the property's irrigation hazard class with SAWS or BSI.
Complete the annual backflow test with a licensed BPAT when the assembly is in the SAWS program.
If the site meets the large-landscape thresholds, complete the separate annual irrigation checkup on time as well.

fireLine.summary: SAWS fire line work is stricter than a normal domestic test. Fireline assemblies must be tested by a licensed fireline tester, and some right-of-way installations stay under SAWS operational control.
highlights: A licensed fireline tester is required for fireline assembly testing.
Assemblies located in the public right-of-way may stay under SAWS inspection and maintenance rules.
Fireline failures do not pause the delinquency clock; the annual record is still unresolved until the assembly passes.
workflowSteps: Confirm whether the fireline assembly is customer-side or in a SAWS-controlled right-of-way location.
Use a licensed fireline tester rather than a generic backflow-only contractor.
Make sure the passing result reaches the SAWS and BSI workflow before the deadline window closes.
Use the SAWS program page to confirm whether the assembly is part of the annual testing program.
Check compliance or find a registered testing company through the BSI customer portal.
Complete testing and make sure the tester submits results through the correct BSI channel.
For new service or construction, complete the CSI and Development Services steps alongside assembly requirements.
failureHighlights: Annual testing is enforced under both local ordinance and state regulations.
SAWS separates customer-side and public-system-side assembly oversight, so scope matters.
New construction and CSI workflows can add extra sequencing beyond the recurring annual test.

costBand.testingRange: Private San Antonio market quotes vary by assembly type and scope.
costBand.repairRetestRange: Repair and retest costs depend on whether the issue is on an external or internal assembly and whether permitting is implicated.
costBand.pricingNotes: Use the SAWS program for compliance path and BSI for tester discovery rather than assuming one standard retail price.

sources.label: SAWS cross-connection control and backflow prevention program
SAWS annual testing of backflow prevention assemblies
